params.name: Feather of Justice
params.description: In ancient Egyptian mythology, Ma'at is often depicted as a goddess with an ostrich feather on her head, which symbolizes truth and justice. In the beliefs of the ancient Egyptians, after death, the heart of the deceased would be weighed on a scale to judge their actions in life. If the heart was heavier than the feather, it indicated that the person had committed bad deeds, and Anubis would hand them over to Ammit to be devoured. Those devoured by Ammit could not enter Aaru and would never find …
